title: Microsoft Q2 FY2026 Earnings: Revenue $69.6 Billion
content: REDMOND, Wash. — Microsoft Corporation today announced the following results for the quarter ended December 31, 2025, as compared to the corresponding period of last fiscal year. Revenue was $69.6 billion, increasing 12%. Operating income was $31.7 billion, increasing 17%. Net income was $24.1 billion, increasing 10%. Diluted earnings per share was $3.23, increasing 10%. Satya Nadella, chairman and CEO of Microsoft, said: 'Our AI transformation is fundamentally changing how work gets done. Azure AI services grew 33% and we now have 85 million Copilot monthly active users.' The Intelligent Cloud segment contributed $25.5 billion in revenue, led by Azure. GitHub Copilot reached 1.8 million paid enterprise seats. Microsoft 365 Commercial products and cloud services revenue grew 15%, driven by Copilot adoption and ARPU expansion. Remaining performance obligation (RPO) stands at $298 billion, up 21% year-over-year, providing strong revenue visibility. The company returned $9.0 billion to shareholders in the form of share repurchases and dividends in the quarter. Microsoft raised its quarterly dividend to $0.83 per share, marking the 17th consecutive year of dividend increases.
